summary: The agent concludes that selinexor is clinically effective for multiple myeloma based on inclusion in current standards of care and specific trial data supporting outcomes in difficult-to-treat patient groups. It highlights the BOSTON trial’s demonstration of effectiveness when used in conjunction with bortezomib and dexamethasone.
